title: Famous Fans of Eva
wikitext:
[]: Famous Fans of Eva             Quote Post by Director Black » Wed Jul 20, 2016 7:00 pm I know Robin Williams said he was a huge one, but is there anyone celebrity beside him that's also a fan?

[]: Re: Famous Fans of Eva             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Wed Jul 20, 2016 8:37 pm Wes Anderson He also included EOE on his list of top 10 Animated Films (that's ALL animated movies, not just anime) for Time Out New York.

[]: Re: Famous Fans of Eva             Quote Post by Director Black » Thu Jul 21, 2016 6:11 pm Guy Nacks wrote: Wes Anderson He also included EOE on his list of top 10 Animated Films (that's ALL animated movies, not just anime) for Time Out New York.

[]: Re: Famous Fans of Eva             Quote Post by Lavinius » Thu Jul 21, 2016 9:45 pm Corneliu Zelea Codreanu famously watched two episodes each day, twice- once in the morning just after waking up, and once with his death squad shortly after dinner. He once said, "It's good to watch Eva episodes in pairs. Almost always the episodes are innately connected and clearly meant to be viewed together as two halves of a whole, and if not they at least agree in tone and are not disagreeable with each other. As for watching them two times a day, the first time, alone, is to get my mind flowing and give me something to motivate me towards and over the day; while the second time is to reëxamine the episodes with my new theories in mind and discuss them with my friends. I must say, though, that on the eleventh and twelfth days of each cycle I grow discontented. It's clear that 22, 23, & 24 form an ideal triad, while 21 stands alone, and is far more related to the previous than to the following. I have often discussed realigning the schedules to take account of this with Horia, but as you know we are very busy and it's difficult to ensure we could always spare the extra hour on the twelfth day. But lately he's been saying that he has an idea burdgeoning, and I'm finally becoming hopeful. After all, as long as one is still alive, one can find happiness." Sadly, Mr. Codreanu was arrested and murdered before the adjustment could at last be made.

[]: Re: Famous Fans of Eva             Quote Post by Xxx_Generic Name_Xxx » Sat Jul 23, 2016 2:31 am Lavinius wrote: Corneliu Zelea Codreanu famously watched two episodes each day, twice- once in the morning just after waking up, and once with his death squad shortly after dinner. But lately he's been saying that he has an idea burdgeoning, and I'm finally becoming hopeful. After all, as long as one is still alive, one can find happiness." Sadly, Mr. Codreanu was arrested and murdered before the adjustment could at last be made.
